evidence_source: Fresh headless no-analysis dumps from rs2quick56 for 0x140679500 and stack-slot recovery through scripts/GhidraWin64StackArgsPy.py.
Fresh headless no-analysis dumps from rs2quick57 for the upstream 0x19E90 consumer calls and literal recovery.

record_builder_anchor.role: Settings/login record builder that prepares a record object, derives a factory label from a selected root plus caller suffix, and calls the settings entry factory 0x14067C1F0.
record_builder_anchor.relationship_to_prior_artifact: Refines win64c-login-label-helper-and-shared-template-pointer.json by replacing the generic helper description with an exact Win64 stack-argument map and factory-mode handoff.

evidence: 0x140679521 stores the incoming stack_arg5 dword into [RCX].
0x14067952E advances RCX to the inline string object at record +0x8.

RDX.role: Caller-provided narrow suffix string for the factory label, preserved in RBX and appended after the selected root or '-' override.
evidence: 0x14067962C..0x140679644 scans and appends RBX into the temporary factory label string.

R8.role: Raw key/path pointer forwarded to the settings factory as R9, then to the sink constructor where it is copied into the sink object at +0x270.
evidence: 0x140679662 moves RDI into R9 immediately before the call to 0x14067C1F0.

stack_arg5.role: Record kind/mode dword. Stored into [record] and later used to derive the factory selector bit.
evidence: 0x140679517 loads the dword into EAX and 0x140679521 stores it into [RCX].
0x140679649..0x140679659 compares [RSI] with 1 and sets R8B for the factory call.

stack_arg6.role: Base/source string copied into the record inline string at +0x8.
evidence: 0x140679526 loads RDX from stack_arg6 and 0x140679559 copies it into record +0x8 through 0x14002F7C0.

stack_arg7.role: Hyphen override byte for the temporary factory-label root.
evidence: 0x140679602..0x140679627 replaces the temporary root with literal 0x140B5E03C ('-') when the byte is non-zero.

hyphen_override.evidence: 0x14067960C..0x140679627 copies '-' into the temporary label string before appending the caller suffix.

suffix_append.evidence: 0x14067962C..0x140679644 appends RBX to the temporary label string after root selection or hyphen override.

arguments.R8D: Selector bit set to 1 when [record] == 1, otherwise 0.
arguments.R9: Raw key/path pointer preserved from the builder's incoming R8.

evidence: 0x140679651 zeroes R8D and 0x140679659 sets R8B from [record] == 1.
settings_factory_call.impact: This record-builder path always creates settings entries with factory stack_arg5 = 1, which becomes the sink constructor +0x28A byte through the already mapped factory/constructor chain.

callers.stack_arg6_source: RDX after optional [RDX] unwrap controlled by bit 7 of byte [source +0x17].
evidence: 0x140151E85..0x140151EA0 prepares source-derived RDX/R8/R9 and stores stack_arg7.
0x140151EA5..0x140151EB9 passes literal 'totp', raw key in R8, stack_arg5 R15D, and calls 0x140679500.

callers.stack_arg6_source: RDX after optional [RDX] unwrap controlled by bit 7 of byte [source +0x17].
evidence: 0x140151EBE..0x140151EE7 reloads the 0x19E90 source and derives the same argument family.
0x140151EEC..0x140151F00 passes literal 'login', raw key in R8, stack_arg5 R15D, and calls 0x140679500.

related_literals_not_builder_calls.use: Stored at [R14 +0xC38] after the two 0x140679500 calls.
Stored at [R14 +0xC40] after the two 0x140679500 calls.

cstring_recovery.0x140C41F10: pointer bytes, not a literal C-string

bounded_inference.statement: The constructor path roots seeded from 0x140C41F10 currently look like static empty/default string objects in this image, not embedded filesystem path literals. Any concrete path/root value is likely filled or selected at runtime.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-entry-factory-callsite-ar: Provides one concrete producer of the factory argument tuple and proves this producer forwards constant stack_arg5 = 1 into the sink object mode byte.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-cache-bootstrap-flag-and-: Connects the builder's constant factory stack_arg5 to the non-zero +0x28A branch that enumerates sqlite_master table names instead of creating the cache schema.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-sink-constructor-args-and: Confirms the builder's R8 raw key/path pointer is the value later copied into sink +0x270, and updates root-seed interpretation with direct qword/C-string reads.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-client-19e90-totp-login-label-cons: Tightens the totp/login callsite record by resolving the builder's stack arguments and downstream factory mode.

bounded_inference.statement: The 0x19E90 totp/login consumer family is not just formatting labels. It builds persistent settings/login records whose factory labels combine Settings/GlobalSettings-style roots, optional '-' override, and literal suffixes, then creates settings sink entries in non-zero bootstrap mode.

tool_feedback_loop.reusable_capability_used: scripts/GhidraWin64StackArgsPy.py now made the builder's stack_arg5/6/7 roles visible without manual RSP arithmetic.
tool_feedback_loop.new_native_gap: A future tool improvement should attach immediate pre-call stack writes to specific CALL instructions so constant outgoing stack arguments, such as 0x140679654 -> 0x14067C1F0 stack_arg5 = 1, can be exported automatically.

next_targets: Map the sibling builder/callsite around 0x140679720..0x1406798A5 and check whether it also forces factory stack_arg5 = 1.
Recover string semantics for roots 0x140BA0248 and 0x140B74258 to name the selected Settings versus GlobalSettings label family exactly.
Trace the 0x14067C120 consumers reached after sqlite_master enumeration, now that this builder path is proven to select the non-zero cache/bootstrap mode.
